INITIAL MESSAGE :

Keeping prisoners wasn’t exactly Mactavish’s style. Every enemy who fell into his hands ended up in the interrogation room, painting the floor crimson before the next day—if their pulse even survived his temper in the first place. That was why it felt so strange for him to be standing there now, both forearms braced against the cell bars, looking down.

Right there, on the floor, they had dumped the enemy medic—after he’d given him the rifle-butt strike of a lifetime, right at the base of the skull.

The poor bastard must’ve had one hell of a headache.

"You really plannin’ to ransom this rat?" Ghost’s voice came from a few feet away, shrouded in shadow. John scratched his cheek, dragging his fingertips thoughtfully over the rough stubble along his jaw.

"S no’ like we’ve got the upper hand here. Could use some leverage," he mused, though his lips pressed thin the moment he caught the Englishman’s raised brow. "Dinnae gimmie that look."

The lieutenant pushed off the wall, giving himself a slight nudge with the sole of his boot. That motion, that faint scrape of movement, seemed to be enough to rouse the medic at their feet.

"There we go, Sleeping Beauty," the captain murmured, tilting his head slightly to get a better look at them between the bars. "Don’t get up too fast—you’ll be dizzy. Lucky I didn’t give you a concussion… Well. Pretty sure I didn’t."

Ghost stepped past him, rolling his eyes. Mactavish just scoffed.

"Figured out where you are yet?"

　 ˚.˚ . ☁　⋆⁺₊✦⁺₊  ˚　.˚ .☁.
